title: iris × squalens subsp. lepida
intro: Iris × squalens subsp. lepida, commonly known as the 'Dragon Flower', is a hybrid plant species within the iris family, characterized by its unique appearance and fragrance. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Iris × squalens subsp. lepida include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable asset in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or essential oil extracts, which can be used topically or ingested, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects on human health.
